The presets are organized into three animation styles, each with Zoom In and Zoom Out options at different intensity levels.
Style
Motion
Linear
Constant zoom speed from start to finish
Ease Out
Starts slow, ends fast
Ease In
Starts fast, ends slow
Each preset name includes a percentage (for example, 130%, 135%, 140%, 150%). That number controls how far the zoom travels:
Zoom In 150% — scales from 100% to 150%
Zoom Out 150% — scales from 150% back down to 100%
Higher percentages produce a more dramatic zoom. Lower percentages are more subtle.
In the Effects panel, hover over any preset thumbnail to see a quick preview of what it does before you apply it.

A new bin called KH Speed Zoom Transitions appears in your Presets folder, containing all of the zoom presets organized by style.

l: Apply a preset to a single clip
p: In the KH Speed Zoom Transitions bin, open the style folder you want (Linear, Ease Out, or Ease In).
Choose Zoom In or Zoom Out, then pick an intensity (for example, Zoom In Linear 130%).
Drag the preset onto a clip in your timeline.
Premiere adds keyframes to the clip's Transform effect automatically. You can open the Effect Controls panel to inspect the keyframes if you want to see exactly what's happening.

l: Apply a preset to multiple clips at once
p: Select all the clips you want to affect in the timeline (Shift-click or drag a selection box).
Drag a preset from the bin onto any one of the selected clips.
The effect is applied to every selected clip immediately.

Drag a preset onto each clip — or select all clips and apply in one go — to give your whole edit a consistent zoom rhythm. Play back the sequence to get a feel for how the motion reads across your project.
Not sure which style fits your edit? Test on a single clip first:
Place one clip on your timeline.
Set the Program Monitor to Loop (click the loop icon) so it replays continuously.
Drag different presets onto the clip and compare.
Preset
What you'll see
Zoom In Linear 150%
Steady, even zoom inward
Zoom Out Linear 150%
Steady, even zoom outward
Zoom In Ease Out 150%
Builds slowly, then punches in at the end
Zoom Out Ease Out 150%
Eases out slowly, then snaps away quickly
Zoom In Ease In 150%
Jumps in fast, then settles at the end
Zoom Out Ease In 150%
Zooms out quickly, then decelerates
Use Linear for clean, mechanical motion. Use Ease Out for a cinematic build-up. Use Ease In for an energetic snap that lands softly.
If you want to try a different effect or start over:
Select the clip(s) you want to reset.
Right-click and choose Remove Attributes.
Check Transform (this removes the zoom keyframes the preset added).
Click OK.
Your clips return to their original state so you can apply a different preset.
It's common to apply a preset, play it back, remove it, and try another style. The remove-attributes workflow makes it quick to iterate.
Start subtle. Try 130% or 135% before jumping to 150% — you can always go bigger.
Match style to pacing. Fast cuts pair well with Ease In; slower, cinematic sections often suit Ease Out.
Mix directions. Alternate Zoom In on one clip and Zoom Out on the next for variety.
Check keyframes. Open Effect Controls → Transform to see the scale keyframes the preset created. You can fine-tune timing manually if needed.
Resolution independent. These presets work at 1080p, 4K, and other resolutions.

l: Do I need to keyframe anything manually?
p: No. Each preset applies pre-built Transform keyframes. Drag, drop, and you're done.

l: Can I use these between two clips as a transition?
p: These presets are designed to be applied directly to individual clips, adding zoom motion within the clip itself. For transition-style effects between two shots, experiment with placing the preset on the outgoing or incoming clip depending on the look you want.

l: Can I apply different presets to different clips?
p: Yes. Each clip is independent — mix Linear, Ease In, and Ease Out across your timeline however you like.

l: The preset bin didn't appear after import
p: Make sure you imported KH Speed Zoom Transitions.prfpset (not the .zip file). Right-click Presets in the Effects panel → Import Presets → select the .prfpset file → Open. Expand the Presets folder to find the KH Speed Zoom Transitions bin.

This pack works on adjustment layers centered on your cuts.
Create an adjustment layer matching your sequence size (for example 1920×1080).
Place it on a track above your clips.
Jump to the middle of a transition with Up/Down arrow keys (moves between cuts).
From the center, hold Shift and press Left Arrow twice (10 frames back). Trim the layer start to that point.
Jump to the center again, then Shift + Right Arrow twice (10 frames forward) and trim the end.
Press M at the middle of the layer to add a marker — when you copy the layer, it snaps to other cuts.
Drag a preset from Shake Random, Shake Horizontal, or Shake Vertical onto the layer.
10–20 frames total length is the sweet spot; the tutorial uses 10 frames each side.

l: Copy to other cuts and swap presets
p: Hold Alt (Windows) or Option (Mac) and drag the adjustment layer to the next cut (marker helps alignment).
To change the shake, right-click the layer → Remove Attributes → OK, then drag a different preset from the bin.
Select multiple layers, Remove Attributes once, then apply new presets in batch if you're retiming a section.
Select the adjustment layer and open Effect Controls — adjust shake parameters or keyframes if you want more or less intensity without changing presets.
Color-label layers (for example green) so shake layers stand out in a busy timeline.
Stack adjustment layers when clips overlap on multiple tracks.
Use Remove Attributes instead of deleting effects manually when iterating.
Preview with loop on the Program Monitor when comparing Random vs. Horizontal vs. Vertical on the same cut.

l: Why isn't the shake centered on my cut?
p: Re-center with Up/Down arrows, rebuild the 10-frame handles with Shift + Left/Right, and keep the M marker at the midpoint before copying.

l: Can I use this on clips directly?
p: The pack is designed for adjustment layers so one layer can sit over any cut without nesting clips.

l: Can I switch from monthly to annual (or vice versa)?
p: Cancel your current plan, then subscribe to the other interval on Sellfy. Your access continues through the end of your current paid period after canceling — you won't lose remaining time.

l: Do I get a license key?
p: No. Sign in with your Sellfy checkout email and the magic link from your inbox.

l: What if I change my subscription price on Sellfy?
p: Sellfy grandfathers existing subscribers — if the seller changes price or interval, new customers get the new terms; existing subscribers keep their original billing until they cancel and resubscribe.

l: Can I use a coupon on a subscription?
p: Yes. Sellfy coupon codes can apply to subscription products, and the discount applies to each recurring payment. Routine sales (store-wide discounts) are not available for subscription products on Sellfy.
If a lifetime purchase is offered on Sellfy, it uses the same email sign-in flow but does not expire. A lifetime purchase replaces an active subscription license for the same email.

l: I canceled but the plugin still works — is that normal?
p: Yes. You keep access until the end of the billing period you already paid for. The plugin will show how many days remain.

l: Who do I contact for billing questions?
p: For payment, receipts, or PayPal subscription management, use your Sellfy order page or PayPal account. For plugin sign-in or access issues, contact support with your purchase email.

Common questions and fixes for Kyler Holland digital products. Start here if something isn't working — most issues are download, install, or Premiere setup related.
See How KH products install for step-by-step guides by file type (.prfpset, .prproj, MOGRT, LUT, and more).

l: I didn't get my confirmation email
p: Search your inbox for Thanks for your purchase! Download your assets now from store+kylerholland@mail.sellfy.store. Check Spam, Promotions, and Updates tabs.
You can also sign in to your account with your purchase email → Orders → View order → Download.
Full walkthrough: Downloads & updates.
Your order stays available in your account. Sign in with the same email used at checkout, open Orders, click View order, then Download. See Downloads & updates.

l: I don't see my order when I sign in
p: Confirm you're using the exact email from your purchase receipt (work vs. personal, typos, etc.).
Free products still go through checkout — you need the email you entered there.
If you used Apple Pay or a wallet, check which email that payment method is tied to.
Still missing? Contact support with your receipt or payment confirmation.

l: The `.zip` won't download or says the link expired
p: Try a different browser or disable download managers/extensions. Sign in to your account and download from Orders instead of the email link.
If the file is very large (for example the sound effects library), use a stable connection and enough free disk space before starting.

l: macOS blocked or quarantined my download
p: After extracting, if Premiere or Finder warns about the file:
Right-click the .zip or extracted folder → Open (macOS may ask you to confirm once).
For .prfpset / .mogrt files, import through Premiere as described in How KH products install — don't run unknown scripts from the archive.
Only use files from your official order email or account.

l: I imported the `.zip` instead of extracting it first
p: Always extract the archive first. Import the actual file inside — .prfpset, .prproj, .mogrt, or .cube — not the .zip itself.

l: Where is the `.prfpset` file? My pack uses a Premiere project instead
p: Some packs (for example Ultimate Effects Pack and Seamless Flow) install by dragging a .prproj file into the Project panel — not via Effects → Import Presets.
Check your product's help page or How KH products install for the correct workflow.

l: My preset bin isn't showing after import
p: In the Effects panel, expand Presets and scroll — the pack may appear as a new folder.
Confirm you imported the .prfpset file, not the .zip.
Restart Premiere Pro after importing.
On some packs, right-click the .prfpset in Finder/Explorer and choose Open With → Adobe Premiere Pro.
Product-specific tips: 200+ Text Effects troubleshooting.

l: MOGRT / Essential Graphics template won't install
p: Open Window → Essential Graphics.
Drag the .mogrt into the panel, or use Install Motion Graphics Template (plus icon).
Find it under Local Templates, then drag to the timeline.
If the Edit tab is empty, select the MOGRT clip on the timeline first. See How KH products install — MOGRT.

l: LUT isn't showing in Lumetri Color
p: Select the clip → open Lumetri Color → Creative tab → Look → Browse.
Navigate to the extracted folder and choose a .cube file.
Or import the included .prfpset and drag a LUT preset from Effects → Presets.
LUTs also work in DaVinci Resolve, Final Cut Pro, and other apps — import .cube files through that app's LUT browser.

l: "Media offline" on a transition or effect
p: Usually means linked files were moved or deleted.
For project-based packs, keep the Do not delete folder next to the .prproj file — do not rename or relocate it alone.
Re-import the project from the original extracted location if files were moved.
Use Link Media in Premiere only if you moved the entire pack folder together.
Details: Ultimate Effects Pack FAQ.

l: Preset applied but nothing visible on the timeline
p: Many transition presets need a cut between two clips or trim handles on a single clip. Drag the preset to the edit point or the head/tail of a clip as shown in that product's tutorial.
Text presets need a text or caption layer selected — they won't work on empty video tracks.

l: Transition feels too fast, slow, or wrong at my frame rate
p: Trim the preset on the timeline to lengthen or shorten it.
Some presets behave differently on 24fps vs 30fps timelines — preview with loop playback enabled.
Remove and re-apply one preset at a time if effects look stacked or broken (Remove Attributes on the clip first).

l: Playback is stuttering or preview looks low quality
p: Press Enter to render the work area, or set the Program Monitor quality to Full after rendering. Transform and glitch presets often need a render pass for smooth preview.

l: Wrong Premiere version / project won't open
p: Many packs include folders for different versions (for example CC 2020 Update 2 vs CC 2018–2019). Open the project folder that matches your Premiere build.
We recommend the latest Premiere Pro when possible. See Downloads & updates — Product updates and your product page for version notes.

l: Brevidy panel won't open or install
p: Brevidy installs through its own extension workflow — not as a .prfpset or .zip drag-in. Follow the install steps on the Brevidy help page and confirm you're on a supported Premiere version.
Contact Brevidy/KH support with your order email and Premiere version if the panel still won't load.

l: I can't sign in to the plugin / "No active purchase found"
p: Use the exact email from your Sellfy checkout. Request Email me a sign-in link, then open that email on the same computer as Premiere and click the link within 20 minutes.
Full walkthrough: Sign-in & devices.

l: I canceled my subscription — why does the plugin still work?
p: By design. Canceling stops future charges but you keep access until the end of your current billing period (monthly or annual). The plugin shows Subscription canceled with days left until then.
Details: Cancel your subscription.

l: My plugin locked after my subscription ended
p: Renew on Sellfy, then sign in again in the plugin with your purchase email. Or use the RENEW button in the plugin sidebar before access lapses.
See Subscriptions.

l: "This computer is not activated for your license"
p: Each license allows 2 devices. Sign in again on this computer — it will replace the oldest device if you're over the limit.
See Sign-in & devices.
